Algeria 3-3 Austria
Jordan 1-3 Argentina
Colombia 0-0 Portugal

stories.headline: India Captain Shubman Gill Meets Football Legend Ronaldinho in the US
stories.what: India's Test and ODI captain Shubman Gill had a memorable encounter with Brazilian football icon Ronaldinho in the United States, where India are currently on tour. The meeting between two sporting worlds — Indian cricket and Brazilian football — has generated significant buzz on social media. Gill is in the US as part of India's cricket commitments, and the chance encounter with one of football's all-time greats became an instant talking point. The interaction underlines the growing global profile of Indian cricket's new generation of leaders.
stories.lens: Gill's meeting with Ronaldinho is a vivid sign of Indian cricket captains now moving in the same international circles as global sporting legends.
stories.article:
India captain Shubman Gill made headlines off the pitch during India's US tour after meeting Brazilian football legend Ronaldinho, with NDTV Sports reporting the encounter that quickly went viral on social media.

Gill, who has taken over leadership of the Indian cricket team, was in the United States as part of the team's touring commitments when he crossed paths with the iconic former Barcelona and Brazil forward. Ronaldinho, widely regarded as one of the most gifted footballers of his generation, is a global sporting superstar whose appeal transcends sport and geography.

The meeting drew considerable attention online, with fans celebrating the collision of two sporting worlds. For Gill, it was a rare moment away from the intensity of international cricket — though the image of India's cricket captain alongside a football legend also spoke to how much Indian cricket's global footprint has grown.

Gill has been at the centre of India's cricketing ambitions as captain, and his off-field profile continues to rise. Moments like these serve as a reminder that India's current cricket stars are increasingly recognised figures on the world stage, mingling with icons from other sports during their international tours.
key_facts: India captain Shubman Gill met Ronaldinho in the United States
Gill is on tour with the Indian cricket team in the US
Ronaldinho is a Brazilian football icon

stories.headline: India, Australia, Great Britain and South Africa Secure First Women's Cricket Quotas for LA28 Olympics
stories.what: Australia, Great Britain, India and South Africa have officially secured the first quota places for women's cricket at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games, confirmed by olympics.com. Cricket returns to the Olympics at LA28 after a gap of over a century, with women's T20 as the format. India's qualification is notable because it came despite their early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup, reflecting the ICC's ranking-based qualification pathway rather than a single tournament's results.
stories.lens: For Indian cricket fans, this is historic — it means the Indian women's team will play cricket at the Olympics for the first time, a watershed moment for the sport's profile and women's cricket funding in India.
stories.article:
India have officially secured a quota place for women's cricket at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games, with olympics.com confirming that Australia, Great Britain, India and South Africa are the first four nations to lock in their berths for the sport's return to the Games.

The confirmation is a landmark moment for Indian women's cricket. Cricket makes its return to the Olympics at LA28 after more than a century, and India's inclusion as one of the first automatic qualifiers underscores the team's standing in the global T20 rankings. The qualification pathway, based on ICC rankings rather than a single tournament result, meant India's early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup — where they were eliminated by Australia — did not derail their Olympic ambitions.

The Indian Express reported that India are among the automatic qualifiers, while West Indies did not make the cut under the current qualification structure. The absence of a powerhouse Caribbean side and the presence of Great Britain — who field a combined England, Scotland and Wales side — reflects how the ICC and IOC have shaped the field for the inaugural Olympic women's T20 competition.

For the broader cricket ecosystem in India, the development carries enormous significance. An Olympic appearance for the women's team will attract new sponsorships, viewership and institutional support. Every ICC-sanctioned T20 international from here on will carry Olympic ranking points, meaning the stakes in women's cricket fixtures have risen sharply — a reality that will reshape how the BCCI and selectors approach bilateral series and tournaments in the years ahead.
key_facts: Australia, Great Britain, India and South Africa are first four LA28 women's cricket qualifiers
Confirmed by olympics.com
India qualified despite early T20 World Cup exit
West Indies did not qualify under current pathway
Cricket returns to Olympics at LA28 after over a century

stories.headline: Cricket's Road to LA28: How the Olympics Qualification Pathway Works — and Where India Stands
stories.what: With cricket confirmed for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, The Indian Express has laid out how the qualification pathway works and where teams like India stand. India are among the automatic qualifiers, while notable sides such as West Indies have not made the cut under the current ICC-backed structure. The format for LA28 is women's T20 cricket, and ICC rankings play a central role in determining who earns the coveted Olympic berths, meaning every bilateral T20 series going forward carries Olympic relevance.
stories.lens: For Indian fans, understanding this pathway matters because it explains why the national women's team's day-to-day T20 schedule now has direct Olympic consequences — every series win or loss shapes India's standing on the road to LA 2028.
stories.article:
The road to cricket's return to the Olympics runs through ICC T20 rankings, and The Indian Express has mapped out exactly how teams will qualify for the women's tournament at the 2028 Los Angeles Games — a pathway that has already produced its first confirmed qualifiers and some notable absentees.

India are confirmed as one of the automatic qualifiers, their ICC ranking securing them a place among the first four nations — alongside Australia, Great Britain and South Africa — to lock in Olympic berths. The West Indies, historically one of the powerhouses of women's T20 cricket, are not among the qualifiers under the current structure, a development that reflects shifting rankings in the women's game.

The qualification system means that ICC-sanctioned T20 internationals from here on carry Olympic ranking points. As The Indian Express explained, every T20 game India play now affects their cricket hopes at LA28 — making bilateral series that might once have seemed low-stakes into genuinely consequential fixtures. Teams that miss out on automatic qualification will look to subsequent qualification events to secure the remaining spots in the Olympic field.

For India, the confirmation of their place is a cause for celebration, even as the Women's T20 World Cup campaign ended in disappointment. The bigger picture is an Olympic debut for Indian women's cricket — a moment that promises to transform the sport's visibility, commercial appeal and grassroots participation across the country in the years between now and the Games.
key_facts: India are automatic qualifiers for LA28 women's T20 cricket
West Indies did not qualify under current pathway
Format is women's T20 cricket at LA28
ICC rankings determine automatic qualification
Every T20 international now carries Olympic ranking points

stories.headline: Australia Knock India Out of Women's T20 World Cup with Record Chase
stories.what: Australia eliminated India from the Women's T20 World Cup with a record-breaking chase, ending India's campaign at the tournament. The defeat was comprehensive, with Australia overhauling India's total in what has been described as a record chase in the competition's context. The loss ends India's hopes of going deep in the tournament but, crucially, does not affect their path to the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, for which they have already secured a quota spot.
stories.lens: India's Women's T20 World Cup exit stings for fans, but the Olympics qualification cushion means the team's biggest goal in women's cricket is already secured — making this a painful but not catastrophic result.
stories.article:
Australia defeated India in the Women's T20 World Cup with a record chase, dumping the Indian side out of the tournament in a result that underlined Australia's continued dominance of the women's game. The defeat was described by The Guardian as a 'record chase', signalling that Australia surpassed a historically significant total to seal the win and send India home early.

For India, the loss continued a difficult campaign at the tournament. The team had already suffered a shock defeat to Ireland — a result that drew sharp criticism from cricket observers — and Australia's clinical performance compounded their misery. The Indian batting and bowling units both came under scrutiny, and the exit raised questions about squad depth and preparation ahead of the marquee events on the horizon.

Legendary opener Sunil Gavaskar, analysing India's earlier loss to Ireland, pointed to two specific reasons for India's failures in the tournament — though the broader pattern of inconsistency was evident throughout their campaign. The team's inability to defend totals or chase them under pressure became a recurring theme.

Despite the early exit, there is a significant silver lining. India have already secured their quota place for the 2028 LA Olympics, where women's T20 cricket will make its Games debut. The World Cup disappointment will now serve as a lesson as the team looks to rebuild ahead of that historic occasion.
key_facts: Australia beat India with a record chase in the Women's T20 World Cup
India eliminated from the tournament early
India had previously lost to Ireland in the same competition
India's Olympic qualification for LA 2028 remains unaffected

stories.headline: New OTT releases: Blast to Baahubali: The Torchbearer headline fresh Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu and Kannada arrivals
stories.what: A fresh slate of South Indian language films is arriving on OTT platforms this week, with titles spanning Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu and Kannada. Among the notable releases are Blast and Baahubali: The Torchbearer, continuing the legendary franchise's presence on streaming. The week reflects the sustained momentum of regional cinema on digital platforms, where audiences now regularly cross language barriers to discover new content.
stories.lens: With Baahubali: The Torchbearer joining the OTT slate, South Indian streaming content continues to dominate platform watchlists — a trend reshaping how all of India consumes entertainment.
stories.article:
OTT platforms are welcoming a diverse crop of South Indian language films this week, with releases in Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu and Kannada arriving to fill up subscribers' watchlists. Among the most anticipated titles is Baahubali: The Torchbearer, the latest extension of the franchise that redefined the scale of Indian cinema, alongside the action film Blast.

The arrival of a Baahubali-branded title on streaming is a notable moment, given the original films' almost unparalleled cultural footprint. The franchise, which broke box-office records and introduced global audiences to the ambition of Telugu cinema, continues to command deep audience loyalty — making any new chapter or spin-off a significant streaming event.

Beyond the headline titles, the week's slate reflects the broader health of regional Indian cinema's digital afterlife. Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu and Kannada films now routinely find large viewership on OTT platforms well beyond their home markets, driven by subtitle accessibility and platform algorithms that surface regional content to new audiences.

For streaming platforms competing fiercely for subscriber attention, a strong South Indian content slate remains one of the most reliable levers. Viewers can expect the full range of genres this week — from action and drama to the kind of ambitious storytelling that has made South Indian cinema a dominant force in Indian entertainment.
key_facts: Baahubali: The Torchbearer is among the new OTT arrivals this week
Blast is another title in the new South Indian OTT releases
Releases span Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu and Kannada languages

stories.headline: Laapataa Ladies actor Satendra Soni alleges filmmaker threatened to kill him on set
stories.what: Actor Satendra Soni, known for his role in the acclaimed film Laapataa Ladies, has alleged that a filmmaker threatened to kill him on set, sparking a serious controversy in Bollywood. The claim has drawn swift reaction from the film industry, with a prominent film body backing Soni and demanding that an FIR be registered against the filmmaker. The allegations, if proven, would mark a grave breach of professional conduct on a film set.
stories.lens: Death threats on film sets put a spotlight on power imbalances in Bollywood — and whether industry bodies can actually protect working actors.
stories.article:
Actor Satendra Soni has alleged that a filmmaker threatened to kill him on a film set, triggering a sharp response from within the Hindi film industry. Soni, who gained wider recognition through Kiran Rao's Laapataa Ladies, made the allegations public, detailing what he described as a life-threatening confrontation during the course of a professional engagement.

The Film Writers Association or a related film body swiftly stepped in to back Soni, issuing a statement of support and formally seeking the registration of an FIR against the accused filmmaker. The intervention signals that industry guilds are increasingly willing to take a public stand in disputes involving threats or intimidation on set — a significant shift from the traditionally insular world of Hindi cinema.

The filmmaker against whom the allegations have been levelled has not been publicly named in the available reports. The demand for a police complaint, however, suggests Soni and his supporters intend to push the matter through legal channels rather than settle it internally.

The incident puts a harsh spotlight on working conditions for actors — especially those outside the top tier of stardom — and raises questions about accountability behind the camera. It comes at a time when Bollywood has faced renewed scrutiny over professional conduct following several high-profile cases in recent years. How law enforcement and the broader industry respond will be closely watched.
key_facts: Satendra Soni alleges a filmmaker threatened to kill him on set
Soni is known for his role in Laapataa Ladies
A film body has backed Soni and demanded an FIR against the filmmaker

stories.headline: Harshad Chopda reveals his girlfriend cheated on him, says he dealt with heartbreak for 16 years
stories.what: Television actor Harshad Chopda has made a deeply personal revelation, disclosing that his girlfriend cheated on him and that he spent 16 years dealing with the emotional fallout of that heartbreak. The candid confession from one of Indian television's most popular and private stars has generated significant attention, given how rarely Chopda speaks about his personal life. The disclosure is likely to deepen the connection he shares with his devoted fanbase, who have long been curious about the man behind his on-screen roles.
stories.lens: Harshad Chopda's rare personal disclosure resonates with millions of fans who follow him closely — and reflects a wider trend of TV stars opening up to humanise their public image.
stories.article:
Harshad Chopda, one of Indian television's most sought-after actors, has spoken with rare candour about his personal life, revealing that a girlfriend's infidelity left him nursing heartbreak for 16 years. The actor, known for his intense performances and a reputation for guarding his private life closely, made the disclosure in an interview with The Indian Express.

Chopda said that his girlfriend cheated on him, an experience that cast a long shadow over the years that followed. The admission of a 16-year emotional journey speaks to the depth of the impact — and to a resilience that his fans say they recognise in his on-screen portrayals of complex, wounded characters.

For a star who commands one of the most passionate followings in Indian television, the revelation is significant precisely because it is so unusual. Chopda has typically kept personal matters out of the public domain, making this disclosure all the more striking. Fan communities across social media responded with an outpouring of support following the interview's publication.

The interview reflects a broader shift in how Indian celebrities engage with the public — moving away from carefully curated images toward more authentic, vulnerable storytelling. For Chopda, the admission may also signal a new chapter, both personally and professionally, as he continues to be one of the most watched faces on Indian television.
key_facts: Harshad Chopda says his girlfriend cheated on him
He dealt with the heartbreak for 16 years
Chopda is known for rarely speaking about his personal life

stories.headline: Shah Rukh Khan's Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa co-star refused to play his sister: 'I was in love'
stories.what: A co-star from Shah Rukh Khan's beloved 1994 film Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa has revealed that she refused to play his on-screen sister because she was personally in love with him. The candid admission throws a charming light on the off-camera dynamics during the making of one of SRK's most cherished early films, which is widely credited with cementing his image as the relatable, romantic hero. The disclosure has resurfaced fan nostalgia for a film that remains a cult classic decades on.
stories.lens: Rare behind-the-scenes revelations from SRK's formative films keep the nostalgia economy around Bollywood's biggest star constantly alive.
stories.article:
A co-star from Shah Rukh Khan's cult 1994 film Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa has made a candid revelation: she turned down the role of his sister in the film because she was in love with him. The disclosure, shared in a conversation with The Indian Express, adds a warm and unexpectedly personal footnote to the story of one of early SRK's most celebrated performances.

Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa, directed by Kundan Shah, is fondly remembered as the film Shah Rukh Khan himself has repeatedly cited as a personal favourite — a comedy-drama in which he played a lovable underachiever. The film's ensemble cast and its intimate, low-key charm set it apart from the bigger-budget romances that would define his later career, and stories from its making carry an affectionate place in Bollywood lore.

The co-star's refusal to be cast as a sibling figure — owing to real feelings for Khan — is the kind of behind-the-scenes detail that fans of classic Bollywood relish. It speaks to the blurred lines between on-screen chemistry and off-screen emotion that were a quiet undercurrent in the industry's formative decades.

The revelation arrives at a time when SRK's extraordinary comeback — following a string of box-office hits in recent years — has reignited global interest in his entire filmography, prompting a fresh wave of revisiting his older, smaller films. Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa, in particular, has found new audiences through streaming platforms.
key_facts: Co-star says she refused to play SRK's sister in Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa
Reason given: she was personally in love with Shah Rukh Khan
Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa (1994) is among SRK's most celebrated early films

stories.headline: Sukumar calls Satya Dev's Rao Bahadur a rare cinematic experience, says Indian cinema has never seen anything like it
stories.what: Acclaimed director Sukumar, the creative force behind the blockbuster Pushpa franchise, has praised Satya Dev's film Rao Bahadur as a rare cinematic experience, declaring that Indian cinema has never seen anything quite like it. The endorsement from one of Telugu cinema's biggest names carries considerable weight and is likely to generate fresh buzz around the film. Such high-profile praise from a celebrated director can significantly amplify a film's reach and box-office prospects.
stories.lens: When a filmmaker of Sukumar's stature calls a film unprecedented, it can single-handedly shift audience curiosity and streaming numbers for Satya Dev's Rao Bahadur.
stories.article:
Director Sukumar — the visionary behind the record-smashing Pushpa films — has lavished rare praise on Satya Dev's Rao Bahadur, describing it as a cinematic experience that Indian cinema has never witnessed before. The endorsement, reported by The Times of India, is among the strongest a film can receive in the Telugu industry, given Sukumar's stature as one of its most commercially and critically successful filmmakers.

Sukumar's comments zero in on the film's uniqueness, suggesting Rao Bahadur breaks genuinely new ground in storytelling or craft — a claim that, coming from him, is not made lightly. Satya Dev, who has steadily built a reputation as one of Telugu cinema's versatile actors, stars in the lead role, and the film appears to be positioned as a distinct, ambitious offering.

The specific nature of what makes Rao Bahadur unprecedented was not fully elaborated in available reports, but Sukumar's framing — that Indian cinema has never seen anything like it — sets unusually high expectations. In an era where original content competes fiercely with franchise sequels and remakes, such a claim can be a powerful differentiator.

The praise is expected to drive significant attention to the film, both in theatres and on the OTT platforms where regional Indian films now reach pan-India audiences. For Satya Dev, the backing of Sukumar's name adds a major boost to what could become one of the more talked-about Telugu releases of the year.
key_facts: Director Sukumar praises Satya Dev's film Rao Bahadur
Sukumar says Indian cinema has never seen anything like it
Sukumar is the director behind the blockbuster Pushpa franchise
